name: CTransformers (GGML/GGUF LLM Inference Python Bindings)

performance.notes: CPU inference; GPU via CUDA/Metal; speed depends on quantization

agent_readiness.notes: Python bindings for C Transformers (GGML/GGUF format LLMs). from ctransformers import AutoModelForCausalLM. llm = AutoModelForCausalLM.from_pretrained('marella/gpt-2-ggml'). llm('AI is') → generated text. llm('text', max_new_tokens=100, temperature=0.8). Streaming: for text in llm('text', stream=True): print(text). Models from HuggingFace: AutoModelForCausalLM.from_pretrained('TheBloke/Llama-2-7B-GGUF', model_file='llama-2-7b.Q4_K_M.gguf'). GPU: gpu_layers=50. LangChain: CTransformers(model='path', model_type='llama'). Supported: LLaMA, GPT-2, GPT-J, GPT4All-J, GPT-NeoX, StableLM, MPT, StarCoder, Falcon, Dolly. Note: Development stalled in 2023 — llama-cpp-python is actively maintained and preferred.

gotchas: Development effectively stalled since late 2023 — no longer actively maintained
llama-cpp-python is the maintained alternative with same GGUF support
GGML format deprecated in favor of GGUF — ensure model format compatibility
GPU support less reliable than llama-cpp-python
best_when: Simple LangChain integration via CTransformers connector
avoid_when: Production inference — use vLLM or Ollama
summary: CTransformers provides Python bindings for GGML/GGUF LLMs. AutoModelForCausalLM.from_pretrained('TheBloke/Model-GGUF', model_file='model.Q4_K_M.gguf'). Streaming, GPU layers, LangChain integration. MIT. Development stalled 2023. llama-cpp-python is the actively maintained alternative with better GGUF support. Avoid for new projects — use llama-cpp-python or Ollama instead.
